channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Resilience is essential as demands on government networks multiply
item.description: Agencies can’t predict the next attack, new mandates or the next cloud outage. But they can control the strength of their network foundation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal CIO outlines ‘one government’ blueprint for overhauling digital services
item.description: Federal Chief Information Officer Greg Barbaccia said his top priority is to get the federal government to “obsess over delivering tangible outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Forward Networks launches agentic AI system built on network digital twin
item.description: Network operations teams face a flood of AI-powered tools promising simplified management through natural language interfaces. Many amount to conversational wrappers around existing capabilities, offering convenience but not fundamental changes to how networks are managed and verified.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco (Nasdaq:CSCO) is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Cisco is focusing on strategic AI initiatives and partnerships across various regions to build and power AI data centers and ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NASA delays the first Artemis moonshot with astronauts because of extreme cold at the launch site
item.description: NASA has delayed astronauts' upcoming trip to the moon because of near-freezing temperatures expected at the launch site.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suse offers cloud sovereignty assessment tool
item.description: Suse has unveiled a Cloud Sovereignty Framework Self Assessment tool, with the intention of helping customers understand the gaps in their compliance with the 2025 EU Cloud Sovereignty Framework.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New PDF compression filter will save space, need software updates
item.description: Brotli is one of the most widely used but least-known compression formats ever devised, long incorporated into all major browsers and web content delivery networks (CDNs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why a lack of governance will hurt companies using agentic AI
item.description: Businesses are acting fast to adopt agentic AI—artificial intelligence systems that work without human guidance—but have been much slower to put governance in place to oversee them, a new survey shows. That mismatch is a major source of risk in AI adoption.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Apple touts ‘unparalleled’ protection for M5 Macs
item.description: Apple overnight updated the Apple Platform Security guide, its Bible for everyone involved in Apple security. The new edition confirms that M5 Macs now benefit from rock solid protection that should protect them against some of the most sophisticated attacks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to reduce Windows driver bloat
item.description: Over time on Windows systems, it’s inevitable that old device drivers get supplanted by new ones. Even on systems where drivers aren’t rigorously maintained, Windows Update will typically update at least a dozen drivers annually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What is prompt engineering? The art of AI orchestration
item.description: Prompt engineering is the process of crafting inputs, or prompts, to a generative AI system that lead to the system producing better outputs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big Tech CEOs’ tortured statements on ICE in Minnesota show an industry captured by Trump
item.description: On the morning of January 24 in downtown Minneapolis, an ICE agent shot and killed protester Alex Pretti, an ICU nurse at a local Veterans Affairs hospital. Just 2 miles away, on January 7, another ICE agent had shot and killed Nicole Renee Good, a mother.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta posts stronger-than-expected Q4 results though costs continue to soar
item.description: Meta’s fourth-quarter results jumped past Wall Street’s expectations thanks to solid advertising revenue, sending shares higher in after-hours trading Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft launches its second generation AI inference chip, Maia 200
item.description: Signaling that the future of AI may not just be how many tokens an AI model generates, but how optimally it does so, Microsoft has announced Maia 200, which it described as a breakthrough inference accelerator and inference powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Fast Pair flaw lets hackers hijack headphones
item.description: Google responds to WhisperPair Fast Pair security flaws by releasing patches to manufacturers and updating certification requirements for protection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
